Q1: a. Yes, it is statistically significant
Q2: {"stem": "Select ALL statements that best explain why the result in Q1 is statistically significant.", "options": [{"text": "p = 0.025 < 0.05 in a two-tailed two-sample t-test, so we reject H0 of no mean difference.", "key": true, "rationale": "Correct p-value logic tied to the stated procedure; reflects sound statistical interpretation (FoI3S).", "misconception_tag": "p_threshold_correct"}, {"text": "A 95% confidence interval for the mean difference would exclude 0, which indicates significance at the 5% level.", "key": true, "rationale": "Correct CI–test equivalence at α = 0.05; shows understanding of inferential quantities (FoI3S).", "misconception_tag": "ci_equivalence"}, {"text": "With n = 1,500 per group, even tiny differences can be detected; the $500 gap is therefore statistically significant.", "key": true, "rationale": "Acknowledges high power from large n and the possibility of small effects being statistically detectable; connects context to inference (FoI4 nuance within a brief reason).", "misconception_tag": "large_n_inflates_sig"}, {"text": "$500 is a big enough difference by itself, so it must be statistically significant.", "key": false, "rationale": "Confuses practical magnitude with statistical significance; ignores sampling variability (FoI1).", "misconception_tag": "practical_vs_stat_confusion"}, {"text": "p = 0.025 means there is a 97.5% chance athletes earn more than non‑athletes.", "key": false, "rationale": "Misinterprets p as the probability the hypothesis is true rather than extremeness under H0 (FoI2S).", "misconception_tag": "p_as_prob_of_hypothesis"}, {"text": "Since the groups were otherwise identical, any observed difference must be significant.", "key": false, "rationale": "Deterministic reasoning that ignores random error and hypothesis testing (FoI1).", "misconception_tag": "determinism_no_variability"}, {"text": "Because the sample is nationally representative, sampling error is negligible, so any observed difference is statistically significant.", "key": false, "rationale": "Conflates representativeness with zero sampling error; Q3: {"stem": "Select ALL statements that best justify how to interpret and use this statistically significant result in context.", "options": [{"text": "The observed $500 difference is likely of limited practical importance relative to typical salaries; SG: {"waypoints": [{"id": "FoI4", "credit": 3, "rule": "Q1 = b AND [Q2 selections include at least one of {Q2.1, Q2.2}] AND [Q3 selections include at least two of {Q3.1, Q3.2, Q3.6}, with at least one of {Q3.1, Q3.2}]."}, {"id": "FoI3S", "credit": 2, "rule": "Q1 = b AND [Q2 selections include at least one of {Q2.1, Q2.2}] AND NOT (meets FoI4) AND [no misconceptions selected from {Q2.3, Q2.4, Q2.5, Q2.6, Q3.3, Q3.4, Q3.5, Q3.7}]."}, {"id": "FoI2S", "credit": 1, "rule": "Q1 = b AND ( [Q2 selections include at least one of {Q2.1, Q2.2} AND at least one misconception from {Q2.3, Q2.4, Q2.5, Q2.6, Q3.3, Q3.4, Q3.5, Q3.7}] OR [Q2 selections include none of {Q2.1, Q2.2} AND Q3 selections include at least one of {Q3.1, Q3.2, Q3.6}] )."}, {"id": "FoI1", "credit": 0, "rule": "Q1 = b AND NOT (meets FoI4 or FoI3S or FoI2S) AND [at least one FoI1-type heuristic selected from {Q2.4, Q3.3, Q3.7}]."}, {"id": "FoI0", "credit": 0, "rule": "Q1 = b AND [no options selected in both Q2 and Q3]."}], "notes": "Formal keying uses option indices: Q2.i refers to the i-th Q2 option; Q3.i refers to the i-th Q3 option. Distractors embody common, plausible misconceptions (e.g., CI-sign confusion, small-n determinism, practical=statistical conflation, accept-null), increasing discrimination without cueing. Option sets are homogeneous, concise, and free of grammatical/length cues.

Q1: a. Yes, it is practically important
